manager.positiveIntent: Make sure the runway math actually works — that the choice to step off the career was a real choice, not a slow mistake.
manager.fearedOutcome: Looking up in eighteen months and finding the number is half what we said we needed, and Jamie hasn't said anything yet but the kitchen has changed.
manager.whatItProtects: The version of me that said "you have permission to do this" out loud, and meant it.
manager.userNotes: Loudest at 11pm with the spreadsheet open. Goes quiet on long walks. Triggered hard by Mom's questions, even when she's not asking about money.

protector.positiveIntent: Hold the steady face — for the kids, for Mom on the phone, for old colleagues from the previous career — while the inside is figuring itself out.
protector.fearedOutcome: Nina asking "what is your job now" and not having a clean answer that doesn't feel like a confession.
protector.whatItProtects: The part of me that doesn't yet know what to call the new shape of the days, and isn't ready to say it out loud.
protector.userNotes: Comes online before family calls and before any conversation with Chris. The over-rehearsing of small things — coffee with Pat, school pickup chat — is the tell.

firefighter.positiveIntent: Make the blank doc go away for a minute. Make the "is this working" question wait.
firefighter.fearedOutcome: An afternoon spent with the cursor blinking and nothing actually being decided — the proof, in real time, that the choice was wrong.
firefighter.whatItProtects: An exhausted part that doesn't believe rest is permitted on a non-employed day, even when nothing more would have come from sitting with it.
firefighter.userNotes: Shows up mid-afternoon, almost always after a Pat call or a Mom call. LinkedIn, the news, refreshing the same email. Quietly aware it isn't doing anything.

exile.positiveIntent: Stay on the path that the people who matter approved of, so they don't have to be disappointed.
exile.fearedOutcome: Mom on the phone deciding, without saying so, that this whole thing was a mistake. Dani realizing it before I do.
exile.whatItProtects: (itself — the one the spreadsheet and the composure and the refresh are all built around keeping out of view)
exile.userNotes: Surfaces hardest after seeing an old colleague's promotion announcement, or after a Mom call where she didn't ask about the new thing at all. The flush of "falling off" lasts a few hours.
